title: Git
description: Exposes local Git repository operations as agent tools.
Covers clone, init, status, log, show, diff, blame, stage, commit, stash, branch management, pull, push, and code search.
Runs entirely in-process without requiring a host git installation.
Supports secure authentication via personal access tokens and SSH keys.

git.repoPath.title: Repository Path
git.repoPath.description: Local path to an existing repository, or a remote URL (https://, git@, ssh://). A remote URL is cloned into a temporary directory at pipeline start and cleaned up on exit. Leave blank to let the agent call git.clone or git.init at runtime.

git.authType.title: Authentication Type
git.authType.description: How to authenticate with remote repositories.

git.username.title: Username
git.username.description: Git username for token-based HTTPS authentication.

git.token.title: Token / Password
git.token.description: Personal access token or password for HTTPS authentication. Leave empty when using SSH.

git.sshKey.title: SSH Private Key
git.sshKey.description: PEM-encoded SSH private key content (starts with -----BEGIN ...). Used when Auth Type is SSH.

git.sshPassphrase.title: SSH Key Passphrase
git.sshPassphrase.description: Passphrase for the SSH private key, if encrypted. Leave empty for unencrypted keys.

git.safeMode.title: Safe Mode
git.safeMode.description: Block destructive operations: force-push and force branch deletion. Normal branch deletion is allowed only when the branch is fully merged into HEAD; deleting an unmerged branch requires force=true (which is blocked in safe mode). Recommended for agent use.
